rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Request For Imposition Of Sanctions And For A Recovery Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Peltner¿s Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admissions is granted. Responses are due by November 30, 2011. CCP sections 2030.300(d); 2030.310(h) and 2033.290(d) state that the court shall impose a monetary sanction against any party, person or attorney who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel a further response discovery unless there was substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. The court finds the opposition to the motion was without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Petitioner and his counsel in the amount of $4,830 payable to the Law Offices of Thomas C. Tagliarini within 15 days. Any party requesting to argue the tentative must call the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on 11/16/11.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Peltner's Motion for Summary Judgment of Herterich's Petition to Determine Distribution Rights filed April 1, 2009 is granted. Herterich's Request for Judicial Notice of the Traeg Wills is denied. As to Herterich's Objections to evidence: #1 overruled, #2 overruled, #3 granted, #4 overruled, #5 overruled, #6 granted. Any party requesting to argue the tentative must call the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on 11/16/11.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD C. BERTOLOZZI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Issues For Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required to discuss the procedural status of this petition.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP OF PERSON AND ESTATE

rulings.Case Title: LARRY PAMBID VS. GUADALUPE GAMINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Order Setting Aside Dismissal And Restoring Action To Civil Active List Or In The Alternative, Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: The motion to set aside the dismissal is granted. Bringing the case to trial was impracticable during the nine months in which it was in default, and certainly during the seven months when there was a default judgment in effect. Excluding that time from the calculation means that five years had not run when the Court granted the motion to dismiss. The order of dismissal is vacated, and the case is restored to the civil active list. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the Court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel by 5:00 p.m. on 11/16/11.
